mnemonics: SIMILAR: Similar Instances Make Intelligent Learning And Recognition
K-Nearest Neighbors: Keep Nearby Neighbors for Best Results
keyPoints: Predictions are made based on the similarity to known data points (neighbors).
The value of 'k' (number of neighbors) is a crucial hyperparameter that controls the model's flexibility.
The choice of distance metric (e.g., Euclidean, Manhattan) fundamentally defines what 'similar' means to the algorithm.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to scale/normalize features, which causes distance metrics to be dominated by features with larger scales.
Choosing a value for 'k' that is too small (leading to overfitting/noise) or too large (leading to underfitting/oversmoothing).
Assuming the algorithm will perform well on high-dimensional data without dimensionality reduction (the 'curse of dimensionality').
